title: Left joins are not evaluated correctly  (in some cases)
poc: CREATE TABLE hastag_3 (subj INT, obj INT);
CREATE TABLE hasgeography1_6 (subj INT, obj INT);
CREATE TABLE key_4 (subj INT, obj INT);
CREATE TABLE geo_values (id INT, strdfgeo TEXT);
CREATE TABLE uri_values (id INT, value TEXT);

SELECT u_node.id, u_node.value, u_tag.id, u_tag.value FROM hastag_3 h0 INNER JOIN hasgeography1_6 h1 ON (h1.subj = h0.subj) INNER JOIN key_4 k2 ON (k2.obj = 805306370 AND k2.subj = h0.obj) INNER JOIN geo_values l_geo ON (l_geo.id = h1.obj) LEFT JOIN uri_values u_node ON (u_node.id = h0.subj) LEFT JOIN uri_values u_tag ON (u_tag.id = h0.obj) WHERE (Within(l_geo.strdfgeo, GeomFromText('POLYGON ((-1 -1, 5.12 -1, 5.12 5.12, -1 5.12, -1 -1))',4326))) ORDER BY u_node.id, u_tag.id LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: Incorrect evaluation of left joins in complex queries
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE hastag_3 (subj INT NOT NULL, obj INT DEFAULT NULL);
CREATE TABLE hasgeography1_6 (subj INT NOT NULL, obj INT UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO hastag_3 (subj, obj) VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 3), (3, 4);
SELECT h0.subj, h1.obj FROM hastag_3 h0 LEFT JOIN hasgeography1_6 h1 ON (h1.subj = h0.subj) WHERE h0.obj IS NULL;
